title: Grocery Categories — Pantry Staples List
html:
TL;DR

  • Yahir kept a 22-item shelf-stable grocery categories list in Apple Notes on 2025-02-26 — energy bars, nuts, jerky, cereal, oils, canned soup/seafood/beans, sauces, pasta, frozen meals, vegetables.
  • The list is structured as categories, not specific brands — Yahir cataloging the shape of his pantry, not a shopping run.
  • Written one day before the same date "categories" file and three days before the Peacock Bar incident — sitting in the late-Feb 2025 "money is tight, push protein" window.
  • Pure specimen: no narrative, no checkbox semantics, just a vertical taxonomy of the food groups Yahir keeps on hand.
  • Sits alongside the August camping list and the Feb 2026 Lubriderm list as the logistics-of-self thread — the un-narrated infrastructure notes.

Notes

The list, as captured: energy bars, nuts, jerky, cereal, fruit snack, oil, can soup, tomato sauce, canned beans, canned seafood, creamer, sauce, pasta noodle, pickled thing, jelly, peanut butter, mac n cheese, frozen pizza, frozen meals, vegetables.

Twenty-two items. No quantities. No prices. No store. The keystroke economy of this note ("Pqeqnut butter" with the typo left in) suggests it was thumbed into the iPhone on the spot, possibly while standing in front of his pantry doing inventory or in a checkout line.

The interesting framing is categorical, not transactional. Yahir is not writing "buy these," he is writing "these are the categories that exist in my food life." That distinguishes it from a normal shopping list — it reads more like a stack for the dry-goods cabinet. The shelf-stable bias (canned, frozen, jerked, dried) maps to the financial period: this is the "money is tight, push protein" note's pantry expressed as a list.

It also predates the Austin move (August 2025) by six months — meaning this is the Grants Pass-era pantry, not the Austin one. Worth comparing to a future Austin-era equivalent if one surfaces, to see whether the food categories shift after the move.
